rationale.risk-routing: Autonomy-impact classes allocate LLM use cases to differentiated human oversight tiers.
rationale.hitl-workflow: Risk tiers prescribe approval, validation, monitoring, intervention, and fallback controls.
rationale.org-governance: Organization-wide classification standardizes LLM risk, assurance, and deployment governance.
rationale.design-only: Specifies the matrix and mitigations without implementation or empirical validation.
rationale.general-ai: Framework governs broad LLM systems-engineering applications, not AI-generated code.
rationale.assistive: Autonomy levels include human-led suggestion and approval workflows.
rationale.agentic: Autonomy levels include independently generated artifacts and fully automated decisions.
rationale.metrics: Defines quality and compliance risk classes from autonomy and impact.
rationale.risk-quality: Impact classification substantively covers downstream product quality and assurance.
rationale.risk-overreliance: Analyzes unverified adoption, skill erosion, and reduced critical thinking.
